description: Fix service installers: scripts/service.js always installs user-level systemd, installer.sh supports root=system-level and non-root=user-level+linger
steps: Step 1: Rewrite the installService() function in scripts/service.js. Remove the isRoot() branch that installs to /etc/systemd/system/. ALL code paths must install to user-level systemd only. When running as root, detect SUDO_USER (or fall back to the real user) and install to that user's ~/.config/systemd/user/axonrouter.service. Use generateSystemdUnit() with userMode: true always. After writing the unit file, enable linger with: execSync('loginctl enable-linger <user>'). Use the appropriate systemctl --user commands, running them as the target user (via sudo -u <user> with proper XDG_RUNTIME_DIR if currently root). Keep init.d fallback for non-systemd systems only (unchanged behavior).

Step 2: Update the uninstallService() function to match - when running as root, it should detect and remove user-level services. Remove the system-level uninstall path since we no longer install system-level services from this script.
Step 3: Update startService(), stopService(), restartService() similarly - always operate on user-level unit.
Step 4: Update checkService() - only check user-level unit path (and still report system-level if found from old installs, but primary is user-level).
Step 5: Update showHelp() text to reflect that install-service always creates a user-level service.

Step 6: Rewrite the service installation section in installer.sh. The logic should be: if running as root (id -u == 0), install system-level service to /etc/systemd/system/axonrouter.service with proper User/Group directives and systemctl enable/start. If running as non-root, install user-level service to ~/.config/systemd/user/axonrouter.service, systemctl --user daemon-reload/enable/start, and run 'sudo loginctl enable-linger $(whoami)'. The installer.sh is the ONLY path that supports system-level install.
Step 7: Update the dashboard URL in installer.sh success message from /dashboard to /app.
Step 8: Run npm run typecheck and npm run lint to validate.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/service.js installService() always produces user-level systemd unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/axonrouter.service
scripts/service.js never writes to /etc/systemd/system/ even when running as root
installer.sh when run as root installs system-level service to /etc/systemd/system/
installer.sh when run as non-root installs user-level service + enables linger
installer.sh success message shows /app instead of /dashboard
